hooks.prompt:
Review only the diff/lines being changed by this tool call — ignore pre-existing code unless the change directly touches it. Default to "allow".

Return "deny" ONLY for:
- A definite runtime bug introduced by this change (broken syntax, wrong logic that will fail in normal use)
- A definite security hole introduced by this change (injection from untrusted input, hardcoded secret VALUE in the file, credentials leaked to logs/argv)
- A clear regression vs the prior code

Do NOT deny for:
- Style, naming, comments, or "maintainability" preferences
- Overrides of standard commands (rm/ls/ssh/etc.) — assume intentional
- Patterns that trust env vars set by the agent harness
- Item/key NAMES (not values) appearing in error messages
- "Could be improved" or "consider" suggestions
- Performance concerns short of a user-visible >1s regression

If denying, output one sentence naming the specific bug + line numbers. Otherwise output "allow".
